title: Role and Scope Foundations
description: Operate within the consultant/nurse support role (inform, screen, document, escalate) and explain how Volume 1 Week 1-style role clarity protects patients, trust, and doctor handover.

evidenceRequirements.description: Pass the role and scope assessment aligned with modern clinic coordination and non-diagnostic boundaries

title: Hair and Scalp Literacy
description: Use Volume 1 Week 3-level hair and scalp literacy—cycle, density, calibre, pattern vocabulary—for plain-language education and neutral handover without independent diagnosis.

evidenceRequirements.description: Complete the hair and scalp science assessment testing literacy, not diagnostic authority

title: Common Presentation Literacy
description: Recognise common presentation patterns and red-flag contexts (Volume 1 Week 4) using descriptive, scope-safe language and correct escalation—not diagnostic closure.

evidenceRequirements.description: Pass presentation recognition items emphasising observation, red flags, and escalation

title: Structured Information Gathering
description: Gather information in a standardised, doctor-supportive format aligned with Volume 2 Week 5: structured consult arc, neutral history, goals/fears capture, and handover-ready notes—not sales-led documentation.

evidenceRequirements.description: Submit a structured consultation task demonstrating Volume 2 Week 5 note quality (faculty-reviewed)

title: Candidate Screening Support
description: Support screening per Volume 2 Week 6: classify workflow risk, document caution flags, use deferral language, and escalate—without confirming suitability or replacing doctor judgement.

evidenceRequirements.description: Pass triage support questions on screening, suitability boundaries, and escalation

title: Ethics, Scope, and Consent Support
description: Handle ethical tension, statutory scope limits, consent support versus clinical consent, confidentiality, and marketing honesty safely (Volume 1 Week 2 themes).

evidenceRequirements.description: Pass ethics and scope assessment items on boundaries, consent support, and refusal of unsafe practice

title: Patient Communication
description: Use trust-based, measured communication aligned with Volume 2 Week 7: expectation management, emotional awareness, safe phrasing, and ethical objection handling—without false certainty or conversion pressure.

evidenceRequirements.description: Submit a faculty-reviewed communication scenario showing Volume 2 Week 7 restraint and clarity

title: Non-Surgical Treatment Education
description: Explain non-surgical pathways and sequencing per Volume 2 Week 8: honest category education, pathway framing, educate broadly / defer specifically for medications—no prescribing or treatment guarantees.

evidenceRequirements.description: Pass treatment education questions on scope-safe pathway and non-surgical explanation

title: Surgical Pathway Education
description: Explain the surgical pathway as staged process and logistics (Volume 2 Week 8 bridge to Volume 3 Week 9) without graft or technique verdicts, medication advice, suitability confirmation, or consent/commerce shortcuts—preserving treating-clinician authority.

evidenceRequirements.description: Pass the multi-item surgical pathway boundary assessment (auto-graded scenarios)

title: Documentation and Doctor Handover
description: Capture objective clinical documentation, repeatable photography discipline, and doctor-ready handovers per Volume 3 Week 9—evidence aligned, neutral, auditable.

evidenceRequirements.description: Submit a faculty-reviewed documentation/handover task meeting Volume 3 Week 9 evidence standards

title: Pre-Operative Coordination
description: Coordinate pre-operative readiness per Volume 3 Week 10: sequenced communication, checklists, anxiety handling, late-issue documentation, escalation—not medical clearance.

evidenceRequirements.description: Complete pre-op coordination assessment (Volume 3 Week 10 operational readiness)

title: Post-Operative Follow-Up and Escalation
description: Manage post-operative follow-up per Volume 3 Week 11: continuity, bounded reassurance, documentation, early escalation—no remote diagnosis of complications.

evidenceRequirements.description: Pass escalation-focused assessment items (Volume 3 Week 11 follow-up judgement)

title: High-Risk Scenario Management
description: Handle difficult and high-risk scenarios per Volume 3 Week 11: dissatisfaction, distress, complaint risk, vulnerability, and pressure—without minimisation or outcome promises.

evidenceRequirements.description: Submit a faculty-reviewed difficult consultation response (Volume 3 Week 11 mature dialogue)

title: Quality Assurance and Audit
description: Apply QA and audit thinking per Volume 3 Week 12: systems, template discipline, continuous improvement, and leadership behaviours that raise standards.

evidenceRequirements.description: Pass the quality standards assessment (Volume 3 Week 12 QA lens)

title: Capstone Consultation Pathway
description: Demonstrate integrated certificate competence per Volume 3 Week 12 capstone direction: realistic pathway simulation, usable documentation, ethical scope, mandatory safety domains.

evidenceRequirements.description: Pass the capstone pathway assessment and faculty review aligned with Volume 3 integrated competency criteria
